observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
necessary adjusting entries in the books of the accounts to record the
completed projects with its corresponding accumulated depreciation to
reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
2.13.1 LDRRM Officer, Municipal Budget Officer, and the Municipal
Treasurer determine the actual amount of the unexpended
balances of the QRF and Mitigation –MOOE at the end of each
year upon closing of the accounts;
2.13.2 Municipal Treasurer transfer the determined amount from the
LDRRMF-General Fund to the Special Trust Fund at year end
or at the earliest possible time in the following year; and
2.13.3 Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting entries to record
the transfer of the unexpended/unobligated balance of the
LDRRMF for CYs 2020 to 2024 totaling ₱4,892,487.25 and
record the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in future years
in the pertinent calendar year of incurrence.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
adjusting entries to correct the misclassified asset accounts with its
corresponding accumulated depreciation.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
necessary adjusting entries to derecognize the issued semi-expendable
properties in the amount of ₱6,758,633.00 and to recognize the
pertinent expense or government equity accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
5.9.1 Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant reconcile the
differences between their respective records on a periodic
basis to arrive at the correct real property tax and special
education tax collectible balances; and
5.9.2 Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entry to fairly
present the actual tax collectible on the Statement of Financial
Position based on the certified list of tax delinquencies
prepared by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
adjusting entries to correct the affected accounts presented in the
Financial Statements and record on-going projects in the appropriate
accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the
necessary adjusting journal entry to reflect the correct amount of the
affected accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to undertake
necessary procedures to revert the accounts payable in the amount of
₱600,396.27 to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, which
has been outstanding for more than three years and have no claim,
administrative or judicial, has been filed or which is not covered by
perfected contracts.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
9.9.1 Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entries to correct the
recognition of prior period income in the current year, and
request information/data/report from the MT for year-end
undeposited collections which will only be forwarded the
following year; and
9.9.2 Municipal Treasurer report all collections for the year even
including those due for deposit in the ensuing year.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer
agreed to:
a. Reconcile the discrepancy noted; and
b. Conduct monthly and quarterly reconciliations of their records.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to prepare the
Certificates of Turnover and Acceptance of these completed projects
with information of its acquisition cost, initiate the preparation of deed
of donation and furnish copies of these to the Municipal Accountant for
derecognition in the LGU’s books.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
12.8.1 Inventory Committee conduct a physical count of all PPEs
which shall be the basis for preparing the RPCPPE;
12.8.2 GS Officer and Municipal Accountant maintain PPE Cards and
PPE Ledgers Cards, respectively and reconcile their records
before preparation of the annual financial statements; and
12.8.3 Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer, and Inventory
Committee complete the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the
Accounting Office records as the first step in one-time
cleansing of PPE.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
13.5.1 GSIS AAO update regularly any changes in the basic monthly
salary of all employees in the GSIS online system; and
13.5.2 Municipal Accountant install controls that would prevent delay
in the remittance of withheld amounts to GSIS.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
14.6.1 Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) reconcile
with the records of the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) before
finalization of the 20 percent AR;
14.6.2 Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the MPDO on the fund
transfer of the payment of loans based on the allotted
appropriation;
14.6.3 MPDO and MBO charge expenses within the allotted budget
and regularly monitor the spending against a specific
appropriation; and
14.6.4 End-user support any reprogramming of PPAs with proper
documentation.

We recommended and the HRMO agreed to spearhead the review and
assessment of the Organizational Structure and Staffing Pattern taking
into consideration the absence of recruitment policy, existing number
of manpower complement, and compliance with the daily minimum
wage rate for Region XIII and submit the same to the Municipal Mayor
so that appropriate action can be taken to maximize the potential of the
human resources of the LGU.

We recommended and the LDRRMO agreed to refrain from using the
LDRRMF for payment of administrative costs to ensure that only
disaster risk reduction and management related activities are charged
against the said fund and submit the lacking documents for
transactions amounting to ₱426,025.95 as soon as possible.

We recommended and the LDRRM Council agreed to include in the
LDRRMF Investment Plan during budgeting process, a separate caption
for the list of PPAs to be charged under the unexpended balance of the
LDRRMF of the previous years to ensure that the unexpended LDRRMF
will only be used on PPAs identified in the LDRRMF Investment Plan.

We recommended and the GAD Focal Person agreed to:
a. Reconcile with the records of the Municipal Budget Officer before
finalization of the GAD AR; and
b. Refrain from utilizing the GAD Fund for expenses not allowed and
not within the approved GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and accountable
officers concerned agreed to exhaust all possible actions to prioritize
the liquidation of fund transfers, comply with the procedures for the
proper disposition of dormant accounts, secure documents as
enumerated in Circular No. 2023-008 and submit/resubmit a written
request to Source Agencies to apply for a write-off of dormant
accounts.

We recommended and the Local School Board thru the Focal Person
agreed to:
a. Document the service of copies of the approved SEF Budget to the
Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal
Treasurer within seven (7) days of its approval;
b. Submit to the DepEd Central Office through the appropriate
channels, the quarterly and annual reports on SEF utilization or the
SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1;
c. Furnish the Local Sanggunian of San Benito and the Regional
Offices of the DBM and DILG the same reports in No. 2 within the
prescribed period; and
d. Post on the LGU's website and/or in at least three (3) designated
conspicuous public places consistent with the deadline of the DILG
Full Disclosure Policy.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
21.10.1 Designated personnel or committee secure Deed of Donation
between the Municipality and private owner to establish legal
rights over the land where the MPB was constructed; and
21.10.2 Local Chief Executive issue a directive to all implementing
units to make sure that in future prospect projects, these
would be constructed on land owned by the LGU.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant, together with the Municipal Assessor, to verify their historical data or records that could shed light on the acquisition costs of the properties and, if available, make an assessment so that the necessary adjusting entries shall be made to record all land owned by the Municipality, in consonance with Section 111 of Presidential Decree 1445 and International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 1 and 17.
observations.managementAction: Both offices verified their historical data together with the Municipal Assessor to verify their historical data or records that could shed light on the acquisition costs of the properties and, if available, make an assessment so that the necessary journal entries shall be made to correct the books of accounts.
observations.reason: Drew adjusting entries to correct the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant – Designate to record the said disallowances, with Notice of Finality of Decision, in the books of accounts as receivable pursuant to Section 60 of MNGAS, and COA Circular No. 2009-006 which prescribed the rules and regulations on settlement of accounts; and correct the erroneous journal entries.
observations.managementAction: Drew adjusting journal entries to correct the erroneous journal entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries to report the correct balance of the affected accounts. Further, follow strictly the recording and reporting guidelines for LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: Drew adjusting entries and followed strictly the recording and reporting guidelines for LDRRMF.
observations.reason: Followed strictly the recording and reporting guidelines for LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the errors and ensure that transactions are properly recorded to reflect in the financial statements the actual results of operation for every calendar year and that only actual and valid expenses shall be taken up in the appropriate accounts and be recorded in the period to which these expenses relate.
observations.managementAction: Took up the appropriate correcting journal entries to correct the books of accounts; and adjusted the erroneous journal entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC) to prepare a resolution to be acted upon by the Sangguniang Bayan for the opening of the Special Trust Fund account within the Siargao area, instruct the LDRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to determine the actual amount of the unexpended balances of the QRF and Mitigation – MOOE for CYs 2020 to 2022 and require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the determined amount from the LDRRMF-General Fund to the Special Trust Fund.
observations.managementAction: Drew adjusting entries to report the correct balances of the affected accounts; and the Municipal Treasurer transferred the determined amount.
observations.reason: Followed the recording the guidelines for LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers to record the funds received by the LGU in 1997 to 1999 since these fund transfers were actually received by them as per the Source Agency’s records.
observations.managementAction: Drew the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the liabilities of the agency to NGA due to unliquidated fund transfer.
observations.reason: Recorded the liabilities of the agency to NGA due to unliquidated fund transfer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to adjust the affected accounts and undertake periodic reconciliation of the balances reported to evaluate discrepancies and make necessary adjustments as warranted.
observations.managementAction: Reconciled the records of both the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant and made necessary adjustments to determine the correct amount and draw the adjusting entries.
observations.reason: The MT and MA already started periodic reconciliation of their reported balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer designate and regularly update her cashbooks by recording all disbursements daily and reconciling the balance with the accounting records at the end of the month, and both the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant-Designate make the necessary corrections should there be a difference between these two records and adjust the unrecorded cash balances to reflect correct balances reported in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed the records of both offices and made necessary adjustments to reconcile the records; both offices conduct monthly reconciliation.
observations.reason: Reconciled the records and adjusted the unrecorded cash balances; the municipal treasurer and accountant performed monthly reconciliation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer designate and regularly reconcile their records and conduct monthly and quarterly reconciliations of their records and diligence should be taken by the concerned offices in the recording and the preparation of reports.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed the records and reconciled the discrepancies; both offices committed to conduct monthly and quarterly reconciliations and submit reports.
observations.reason: Conducted monthly and quarterly reconciliations and corrected the records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to monitor and perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter errors and omissions and to reconcile accounts and effect adjustments to reflect correct balances in the financial statements, pursuant to Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Monitored and performed constant review and appraisal of transactions and reconciled the accounts; effected adjustments to reflect correct balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GSO consolidate the issuances, supported with RIS on a monthly basis in the SSMI to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant, who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method as prescribed under Section 121 of the same Manual, and maintain supplies ledger card (SLC) for each item of supplies and/or other inventory items.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed and compared the records of both offices and made necessary corrections; maintained supplies ledger cards and submitted consolidated issuances.
observations.reason: Reviewed and consolidated records and maintained required documentation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to create an Inventory Committee that is composed of two or more employees who will conduct a physical count of all PPEs which shall be the basis for preparing the RPCPPE; the GSO to maintain PPE Cards, and the Accounting Office to maintain PPE Ledgers Cards and ensure reconciliation of their records before preparation of the annual financial statements; and to complete the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the Accounting Office records to validate the accuracy of the PPEs.
observations.managementAction: Created an Inventory Committee; reviewed and reconciled PPE records; prepared RPCPPE and maintained PPE Cards and Ledger Cards.
observations.reason: One-time cleansing and reconciliation of PPE records were conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the personnel-in-charge to thoroughly review the transactions that resulted in the negative balances of the accounts and prepare the adjusting entries to establish the accuracy and valuation thereof.
observations.managementAction: Review the transactions that resulted in negative balances and prepared the adjusting entries to establish the accuracy and valuation of the accounts.
observations.reason: Adjusted entries were prepared to correct negative and abnormal balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the personnel-in-charge of the preparation and submission of the cash advance for Confidential Funds submit the required supporting documents within seven (7) days after release of check, and to support it with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed the procedures and required the personnel to submit complete supporting documents for cash advances within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Compliance with documentary requirements and timely submission were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant and accountable officers concerned to exhaust all possible actions to prioritize liquidation of fund transfers for every project; for long outstanding FTs, if supporting documents cannot be traced, request the Source Agencies to apply for a write-off of dormant accounts, and if granted, immediately submit the required documents for write-off.
observations.managementAction: Reviewed and pursued actions to prioritize liquidation; submitted requests to Source Agencies for possible write-off and to follow-up.
observations.reason: Submitted requests for write-off to source agencies and pursued liquidation actions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that municipal officials closely monitor all disbursements and establish monitoring to ensure disbursements are within appropriations, and that augmentations and realignments of appropriation are fully supported with proper documentation.
observations.managementAction: Monitored disbursements and established procedures to ensure expenditures are within appropriations; required proper documentation for reprogramming.
observations.reason: Established monitoring and proper documentation for augmentations and reprogramming.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and LDRRM Officer on including income collected from other local sources in the monthly fund transfer to LDRRMF; and if still unimplemented, stricter sanctions will be imposed.
observations.managementAction: Coordinated and included the income from other local sources in the monthly fund transfers to LDRRMF; monitored implementation.
observations.reason: LDRRMF monthly transfers were coordinated and updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management not grant benefits or other forms of compensation to any government official or employees except those specifically authorized by law; and compel the concerned employees to refund the excessive amount.
observations.managementAction: Compelled concerned employees to refund the excess amount and followed regulations on compensation.
observations.reason: Employees refunded the excessive amounts; regulations on compensation were followed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the concerned employees to refund the excess amount of ₱55,524.60 and follow strictly the regulations on the grant of compensation-related Magna Carta benefits to PSWs based on their Daily Time Records and/or the actual number of workdays rendered.
observations.managementAction: Employees refunded the excess amount and strict compliance with the daily time records and rules was instituted.
observations.reason: Excess amount was refunded and procedures tightened.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Department Heads concerned immediately prepare a consolidated report on all actions taken relative to the audit observations and recommendations included in the Annual Audit Report for CY 2022 using the prescribed AAPSI Form; and henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed period for the submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA.
observations.managementAction: Submitted the AAPSI on-time and prepared consolidated reports and committed to adhere to the prescribed period for submission of AAPSI.
observations.reason: AAPSI was submitted on time and consolidated reports prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that a Disposal Committee be created and pertinent documents be prepared by the Disposal Committee to facilitate appraisal review of the unserviceable properties, including Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), report of unserviceable properties for disposal, photographs and other related documents; and file the application for disposal with the COA, pursuant to COA Circular 92-386.
observations.managementAction: Disposal Committee was created and submitted required documents; started the disposal process for some unserviceable motor vehicles.
observations.reason: Disposal Committee created; disposal process initiated and documents prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to exert priority in appointing a licensed Certified Public Accountant as Municipal Accountant and to designate personnel equipped with adequate accounting knowledge and competence to discharge the function of an accountant; and publish the vacancy for the position.
observations.managementAction: Appointed a licensed CPA as Municipal Accountant and designated personnel to perform accounting functions; published vacancy as required.
observations.reason: A licensed CPA was appointed as Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC observe Section 47 of RA No. 984 which disqualifies suppliers with conflict of interest by consanguinity or affinity up to the third civil degree to the LCE or any of the LGU’s officers or employees having direct access to the information that may substantially affect the result of bidding including alternative modes of procurement.
observations.managementAction: Restructured the BAC and ensured that no member of the BAC is related to the LCE; observed Section 47 of RA No. 984 and did not award contracts to bidders with conflicts of interest.
observations.reason: BAC was restructured and conflicts of interest were avoided in procurements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management take full opportunity on the application of the prescribed guidelines and procedures set forth in COA Circular No. 2020-006, particularly the conduct of physical count of PPE, the recognition of PPE items found at station, and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, prepare the Physical Inventory Plan to be approved by the LCE and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period, and to comply with the prescribed unique property number indicated in the property stickers used for inventory tagging.
observations.managementAction: Took advantage of COA Circular No. 2020-006, conducted physical count of PPE and prepared required plans and complied with inventory tagging procedures.
observations.reason: One-time cleansing and physical count conducted and inventory tagging implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Engineer create an Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical count of the local road networks and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network not later than January 31 of each year; require the Municipal Accountant to present in the Notes to Financial Statements the required disclosure for local road networks, and to prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for each local road; and require the General Services Officer to prepare the annual Report of Local Road Network.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Engineer started the process of complying with the COA Circular; consolidated reports from other offices and will continue implementation with cooperation of other offices.
observations.reason: Started process of consolidating records and will continue implementation; cooperation with other offices ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to report the correct balance of the affected accounts. Further, follow strictly the recording and reporting guidelines for LDRMMF as prescribed in COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.reason: No one is capable of reconciling the previous entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to monitor and perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter errors and omissions and to reconcile accounts and effect adjustments to reflect correct balances of Due from/to Other Funds and Due from/to Special Accounts in the financial statements, pursuant to Section 111(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts all issuances as expenses, with RIS and SSMI as supporting documents. Further, a supplies ledger card (SLC) for each item of supplies and/or other inventory items shall be maintained to record the acquisition, receipt, and issuance pursuant to the Manual on the NGAS; and the GSO to consolidate the issuances, supported with RIS, on a monthly basis in the SSMI to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant, who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method as prescribed under Section 121 of the same Manual.
observations.reason: No permanent GSO and municipal accountant to do the reconciliation.

observations.reason: Creation of Inventory Committee was not given priority.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review the transactions that resulted in the negative balances of the accounts and accordingly prepare the adjusting entries to establish the accuracy and valuation thereof.
observations.reason: No municipal accountant to review the work of accounting staff.

observations.reason: Reflect the adjusting entries at year-end; it is impossible to liquidate such fund transfer since there were no available records.

observations.reason: On the process of reviewing the balance of receivable arising from disallowances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: (a) a Disposal Committee should be created, (b) pertinent documents should be prepared by the Disposal Committee to facilitate appraisal review of the unserviceable properties by the COA, including a properly accomplished Inventory and Inspection Report for unserviceable properties, appraisal Report of unserviceable properties for disposal, and other related documents such as photographs of unserviceable properties for disposal; and file the application for disposal with the COA, pursuant to COA Circular 92-386.
observations.reason: Creation of Disposal Committee was not given priority.

observations.reason: This was not given priority by the Management.

observations.reason: This was not given priority by the Management.

We recommended the Management require the Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC) to prepare a resolution to be acted upon by the Sangguniang Bayan for the opening of the Special Trust Fund account within the Siargao area, instruct the LDRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant to determine the actual amount of the unexpended balances of the QRF and Mitigation –MOOE for CYs 2020 to 2022 and require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the determined amount from the LDRRMF-General Fund to the Special Trust Fund.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Vouchers to record the funds transfer received by the LGU in 1997 to 1999 since these were actually received by them as per the Source Agency’s records.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to draw the necessary entries to adjust the affected accounts and ensure proper recording of payment, discounts, and penalties for proper presentation of the accounts in the financial statements; and the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to undertake periodic reconciliation of the balances reported to evaluate discrepancies and make necessary adjustments as warranted.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer-Designate to update regularly her cashbooks by recording all disbursements daily and reconciling the balance with the accounting records at the end of the month, and both the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant-Designates make necessary corrections should there be a difference between these two records and adjust the unrecorded cash in bank balances to reflect/establish correct balances reported in the Financial Statements.

Moreover, due diligence should be taken by the concerned offices in the recording and the preparation of reports.

Further, a supplies ledger card (SLC) for each item of supplies and/or other inventory items shall be maintained to record the acquisition, receipt, and issuance pursuant to the Manual on the NGAS; and the GSO to consolidate the issuances, supported with RIS, on a monthly basis in the SSMI to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant, who shall compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method as prescribed under Section 121 of the same Manual.

We recommended the following: (a) create an Inventory Committee that is composed of two or more employees including the GSO who will conduct a physical count of all PPEs which shall be the basis for preparing the RPCPPE, (b) the GSO to maintain PPE Cards, and the Accounting Office to prepare and maintain PPE Ledgers Cards and ensure that reconciliation of their records is done before preparation of the annual financial statements, and (c) that the Municipal Accountant, Property Custodian, and Inventory Committee to complete the reconciliation of the RPCPPE with the Accounting Office records to validate the accuracy of the PPEs.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly review the transactions that resulted in the negative balances of the accounts and accordingly prepare the adjusting entries to establish the accuracy and valuation thereof.

We recommended the personnel-in-charge of the preparation and submission of the required supporting documents for the cash advance for Confidential Funds to submit within seven (7) days after release of check, and to support it with complete documentation.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO), Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), and Municipal Budget Officer (MBO), not charge expenses without appropriations and over the allotted budget, and regularly monitor the expenditures against a specific appropriation to avoid the incurrence of expenses above the budget. Also, ensure that the augmentations and realignment of appropriation are fully supported with proper documentation.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and LDRRM Officer on providing sufficient funds to LDRRMF by including the income collected from other local sources in the monthly fund transfer from GF-Proper to GF-LDRRMF. Moreover, if still unimplemented, stricter sanctions will be imposed.

We recommended that the Department Heads concerned immediately prepare a consolidated report on all management actions taken relative to the audit observations and recommendations included in the Annual Audit Report for CY 2022 using the prescribed AAPSI Form; and henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed period for the submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA.

We recommended the following: (a) a Disposal Committee should be created, (b) pertinent documents should be prepared by the Disposal Committee to facilitate appraisal review of the unserviceable properties by the COA, including a properly accomplished Inventory and Inspection Report for unserviceable properties, appraisal Report of unserviceable properties for disposal, and other related documents such as photographs of unserviceable properties for disposal; and file the application for disposal with the COA, pursuant to COA Circular 92-386.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to exert more effort in giving priority to appointing a licensed Certified Public Accountant as a Municipal Accountant. In the meantime, designate personnel equipped with adequate accounting knowledge and competence to discharge the function of an accountant. As it will be impossible for the MCRO to perform the duties; and direct the Municipal Human Resource Officer to publish the vacancy for the position.

We recommended that in the forthcoming transactions of the Municipality, the Bids and Awards Committee strictly observe the provision of Section 47 of RA No. 984 which disqualifies bidders having a conflict of interest to participate in the procurement of the LGU. Moreover, the management does not award contracts to bidders with conflicts of interest due to relationships by consanguinity or affinity up to the third civil degree to the LCE or any of the LGU’s officers or employees having direct access to the information that may substantially affect the result of bidding including alternative modes of procurement, such as, but not limited to, the members of the BAC, the members of the TWG, the BAC secretariat, and the designers of the project.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Accounting  and  Treasury  Offices  conduct
immediate review of their records and identify the items that cause the
discrepancies between the balance per books and the balance per record and
reconcile their records at least  quarterly  and  effect immediate adjustment
to avoid the accumulation of discrepancies  in order to reflect  correct  and
reliable  Cash  in  Local  Treasury  balance.  Moreover, instruct  the
Municipal  Accounting  Office  to  prepare SLs for the Cash  Local  Treasury
account and trace the accountabilities of the previous and current Municipal
Treasurers.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office:

a. Issues of demand letters to (i) notify the  concerned  officials and
employees of their responsibility to  settle  their  unliquidated cash
advances.  If  settlement  was  not  received,  withhold from their salaries
considering  that  they  have  been  notified  and  given  more  adequate
time  to  liquidate;  (ii) employees who already resigned and  transferred to
immediately cause the liquidation and  settlement  of  the   unliquidated
balances;


b. Requests the write-off of the unliquidated cash advances of  the
deceased employees;


c. Reclassifies “Other Receivable” account to Advances to  Officers and
Employees account in  order  to  present  fairly  the  total balance of
unsettled cash advances;


d. Ensures the completeness of prescribed supporting documents  for the
granting of cash advances in processing claims  for  payment  thereof;


e. Not grants additional cash advance to  concerned  officials  and
employees unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated;


f. Strictly monitors periodically  the  submission  of  liquidation
reports to ensure timely  accounting  thereof  and  avoid  undue
accumulation of outstanding cash advances; and


g. Strictly complies with  the  provisions  of  the  aforementioned
Circulars to avoid penalty stated thereon.

maintenance of stock cards  for  supplies  to  account  for  the
receipt and disposition of the same and prepare Requisition  and
Issue Slip for issuance and the Report of Supplies and Materials
Issued monthly and submit the same to the  Municipal  Accountant
for recording purposes to fairly present its inventory  accounts
in the financial statement;


b. Instructs the Municipal Accountant to observe proper  adjustment
of inventory accounts, prepare supplies ledger  card  per  stock
number and make an internal control policy on the maintenance of
stock cards of the MDRRMO, MTO and MHO to establish authority on
the control of inventory and prepare and submit the distribution
lists to the Municipal Accounting Office monthly for recognition
of expense; and

Committee and fuel them up to religiously conduct  the  required
annual  physical  count  of  unused/undistributed  supplies  and
materials.

We recommended the following:

a. That the Municipal Accountant, Property Custodian, and Inventory
Committee complete the reconciliation of  the  RPCPPE  with  the
Accounting Office records to validate the accuracy of the PPEs;


b. That the General Services Office causes the  immediate  disposal
of unserviceable items and the Municipal  Accounting  Office  to
record the dropping from the books of the items disposed; and

and ensure that reconciliation of their records is  done  before
preparation of the annual financial statements.

We recommended that:

and complete the inventory and accounting of  the  damaged  PPEs due to
the typhoon;

Loss and apply for a relief from accountability to COA; and


c.  The  Accounting  Office  records  the  loss  of  properties  in
accordance with the GAM for LGUs.

Accountant to record  all  the  vehicles  acquired  either  through
purchase or donation to the appropriate  asset  accounts  with  its

transactions pertaining to completed projects  to  the  appropriate

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to device and implement control measures to ensure that the amount granted for communication allowance is reasonable and used for official business with a limit set every month. Furthermore, if cell cards/E-Load shall be allowed, consider purchase in bulk by the GSO, by following the required procurement procedures and distribute the same in accordance with the guidelines.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have reviewed the completeness of the supporting documents, exercise prudence and diligence in the disbursement of funds and strictly adhered to the provisions of payment to ensure that the amount granted for communication allowance is reasonable and used for official business with a limit set every month.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to strengthens the monitoring controls by requiring the Accounting Office, CDRRM Office, RHU Office, and Mayor’s Office to: a. Ensure proper coordination among the Offices concerning the receipt and distribution of Donations In-Kind; b. Ensure issuance of Acknowledgment Receipts to all future receipts of donations in-kind to confirm that donations are properly recorded and accounted for; c. Ensure that all future issuances of donated items are properly supported with Issuance Form for In-Kind Donations or any document/record to prove receipt by and distribution to the intended beneficiaries; and d. Determine the inventory of remaining undistributed items for recording in the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for DRRM.
observations.managementAction: They encountered unavailability of manpower to monitor the distribution. However, the Municipality have started the coordination mechanisms of the concerned offices with regard to the receipt and distributions of donations in-kind.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the MDRRMO: a. Update themselves to the latest policies and guidelines on Donations In-Kind as provided by the relevant and competent government offices; and b. Ensure the on-time preparation and submission of the Report of the Donations Received, Distributed and Balances to the Office of the Civil Defense and Auditor’s Office, in compliance to Section 2(b) of COA Circular 2020-009, moving forward.
observations.managementAction: The Management have been updated to the latest policies and guidelines on Donations In-Kind as provided by the relevant and competent government offices; and submitted the lacking report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the GAD Focal Point System properly plans and revises it if the need arises and regularly monitor the implementation of GAD PPAs and maximize the utilization of the GAD budget to achieve the objective of the government regarding the institutionalization of GAD in the community.
observations.managementAction: They encountered unforeseen challenges during the implementation especially during the pandemic period. And there was no cash back up on the additional projects and activities due to the unrealistic income projected, making them impossible to implement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to stops the practice of splitting the procurement of various goods and services and instruct the General Services Officer or BAC Secretariat, as the case maybe, to consolidate the procurement of similar and related items into one purchase request and subject the same to competitive bidding pursuant to Rule IV, Section 10 of the revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure competitiveness and obtain the lowest and most advantageous price.
observations.managementAction: The Management have stopped the practice of splitting of procurement of various goods and services.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to ensures that sufficient funds are provided to LDRRMF by including the income collected from other local sources in the monthly fund transfer from GF-Proper to GF-LDRRMF and require the LDRRMO to prepare the monthly Utilization Report duly reconciled with the accounting records and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality have started transferring the deficient amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: a. Instruct the MDRRMO, Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accounting Office to determine the actual amount of the unexpended balance of the QRF and Mitigation –MOOE and transfer the said fund to a special trust account; b. Require the LDRMC to revise their LDRRM Plan for CY 2021 and incorporate therein the programs, projects and activities for the unexpended appropriations of QRF and LDRRMF-MOOE of prior years and submit the same to the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO); and c. Require the MPDO to revise the current year’s Annual Investment Program (AIP) in consideration of the revised LDRRMP and submit the same to the Local Sanggunian and LCE for their approval.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO failed to revise the LDRRM Plan for CY 2021. Also, after they transfer the special trust account to another bank, they will be transferring any unexpended balance for the year going forward.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to provide sufficient personnel support to the MDRRMO in order to facilitate the implementation of the LDRRM PPAs, require the LDRRM Officer to install mechanism that will monitor the implementation of the PPAs identified in the LDRRM Plan and fast-track its implementation and formulate or propose programs that are achievable to ensure implementation thereof within the budget year.
observations.managementAction: Additional personnel support was already provided by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Accounting Office installs a scheme to facilitate the timely remittance of withheld amount of different government institutions and should strictly comply with law so as not to deprive the officials and employees of further benefits and to avoid the burden of any surcharges and interests not due to them.
observations.managementAction: Withheld amount of different government institutions were already remitted timely by the Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere to the provisions prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 pertaining to warranty security, not only in infrastructure projects but also for the procurement of goods. Henceforth, ensure that future procurements be deducted with the at least 1% retention money or require a special bank guarantee of the same amount, otherwise any expenses due to the defects within the warranty period be charged to concerned approving officials.
observations.managementAction: Management strictly adhered to the provisions prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 pertaining to warranty security, not only in infrastructure projects but also for the procurement of goods.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer coordinates with the concerned offices in ensuring that local fees and charges are updated regularly, with the end view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The concerned offices were already coordinating in relation to the updating of the local fees and charges, with the end view of increasing the revenue collections of the Municipality..

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to strictly implement actions to strengthen its collection and monitoring procedures and direct Local Finance Committee to undertake careful planning and execution of the proposed annual budget on the basis of reasonable and realistic projection of revenues.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented actions to strengthen its collection and monitoring procedures and directed Local Finance Committee to undertake careful planning and execution of the proposed annual budget on the basis of reasonable and realistic projection of revenues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant: a. Issues demand letters to (i) notify the concerned officials and employees of their responsibility to settle their unliquidated cash advances. If settlement was not received, withhold from their salaries considering that they have been notified and given more adequate time to liquidate; (ii) employees who already resigned and transferred to immediately cause the liquidation and settlement of the unliquidated balances; b. Requests the write-off of the unliquidated cash advances of the deceased employees; c. Reclassifies “Other Receivable” account to Advances to Officers and Employees account in order to present fairly the total balance of unsettled cash advances; d. Ensures the completeness of prescribed supporting documents for the granting of cash advances in processing claims for payment thereof; e. Not grants additional cash advance to concerned officials and employees unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated; f. Strictly monitors periodically the submission of liquidation reports to ensure timely accounting thereof and avoid undue accumulation of outstanding cash advances; and g. Strictly complies with the provisions of the aforementioned Circulars to avoid penalty stated thereon.
observations.managementAction: The unsettled cash advances are already recorded under Advances to Officers and Employees, and recommendations are currently implemented and followed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the MSWD Office to notify the concerned beneficiary and take necessary actions and also for other concerned personnel to thoroughly review the qualified beneficiaries of the any assistance from the government to avoid any errors, duplications and other matters.
observations.managementAction: The Management implemented fully the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: a. Direct the Municipal Accountant to scrutinize carefully the disbursement claims of the leagues of local elective officials for membership dues and contribution and determine if the rates/amounts claimed are strictly in accordance with their respective constitution and by-laws. b. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer not to release payments to leagues without the government prescribed O.R., and; c. Require leagues annual financial and accomplish reports, as a prerequisite for the renewal of membership fees in the ensuing years, to ascertain that government funds have been utilized for lawful and exclusively for public purposes.
observations.managementAction: The concerned League of Municipalities through the SB officials is yet to give the annual financial and accomplishment reports. Follow ups have been made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the concerned Dep-Ed Official to settle/liquidate immediately in full the aforecited cash advances, discontinue the practice of unauthorized cash advances and grant cash advances only to appointed employees duly designated as Disbursing Officer properly bonded pursuant to Section 305 (f) of RA 7160 and Sections 4.1.4 and 4.1.5 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
observations.managementAction: The said outstanding cash advance was already fully liquidated by the concerned officer.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the concerned elected officials to avoid claims of travelling allowances more than what is allowed as stipulated EO No. 77. Further, directs the Municipal Accountant to impose strictly adhere on the implementation of E.O. 77 dated March 15, 2019.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have already imposed strict adhere on the implementation of E.O. 77 dated March 15, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to fast track the implementation on the electrification of power source of the water system in order to reduce the expenditures so that risk of unhealthy financial performance can be addressed. Moreover, require the Local Chief Executive to initiate the review of operations of the Waterworks System as economic enterprise. Also, conduct periodic and continuous monitoring and review of the implementation of strategies with the end view that the desired results are achieved.
observations.managementAction: The management implemented the electrification of power source of the water system in order to reduce the expenditures.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. review all journal entries to ensure that transactions are properly recorded to reflect in the financial statements the actual results of operation for every calendar year; and b. ensure that only actual and valid expenses shall be taken up in the books using the appropriate expenses accounts and be recorded in the period to which these expenses relate.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reviewed the journal entries to ensure that transactions are properly recorded to reflect in the financial statements the actual results of operation for every calendar year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Accounting and Treasury Offices to perform quarterly reconciliation of their records for immediate correction of noted deficiencies and carefully charging of fund disbursements in order to present a reliable financial statement.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting and Treasury Offices to perform quarterly reconciliation of their records for immediate correction of noted deficiencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that upon the completion of physical count of the Inventory Committee, the Local Chief Executive institute measures to properly dispose all obsolete and unserviceable properties pursuant to the directives of Section 79 of PD 1445 and Sections 379 of RA 7160 and in accordance with the procedures cited in COA Circular No. 89-296 to prevent further deterioration and to realize income from the sale thereof.
observations.managementAction: They already requested for disposal and the team notified them of the lacking requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Office of the San Benito Water System Management to prepare the monthly billing summary to be submitted to the Accounting Office as basis in the recording of revenue earned from waterworks system.
observations.managementAction: The San Benito Water System Management prepares the monthly billing summary to be submitted to the Accounting Office as basis in the recording of revenue earned from waterworks system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Budget and GAD Focal Point System to refrain from utilizing the GAD Fund for expenses not allowed and not within the approved GAD Plan and Budget. Moreover, all the disbursements related to GAD Fund should be checked and signed first by the GAD Focal Point System before processing.
observations.managementAction: Due to constraints in the budget, still there were expenses that cannot be directly attributed as part of the GAD Plan and Budget.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: a. Demand from the HRMO and Treasury Office the submission of the missing DTRs of February 2019 and its supporting documents; b. Require officers and employees to submit the payrolls with the approved DTRs and its mandated format by CSC, application for leave and other pertinent supporting documents as required in Sections 1.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001; and; c. Direct concerned personnel who prepares the payroll to exclude those employees, who failed to submit their DTRs, in the payroll to adopt the “NO DTR, NO SALARY” policy and to ensure the prompt submission of their DTRs.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO and Treasury Office submitted the missing DTRs of February 2019 and its supporting documents, and require officers and employees to submit the payrolls with the approved DTRs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: a. Strictly observe the relevant provisions of RA 7160 and CSC-COA-DBM JM No.1 dated June 15, 2017 such that contracts are prepared and/or drawn in the hiring of JO workers, JOs do not perform regular agency functions and specific and measurable work outputs are defined in the contract; b. Observe proper measures in government spending by avoiding the hiring of too many JOs performing unnecessary jobs or works; c. Require the Accounting Office to ensure that JO contracts and the accomplishment reports of JOs are attached to the payroll before payment is made; and d. Ensure that rates of JO workers commensurate to the actual work performed.
observations.managementAction: The Management strictly observed the relevant provisions, and required the employees to submit complete documentation in connection to their payroll.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: 1. Demand from the PHWs the submission of all the required DTRs mentioned above supported with Civil Service Form 48 together with its necessary supporting documents as soon as possible; and 2. Require the payroll in-charged to stop the granting of fix rate of ₱150.00 and ₱1,500.00 for Laundry and Subsistence Allowance, respectively, and follow strictly the rules and regulations on the grant of compensation-related Magna Carta benefits to PHWs and MSWDO based on their Daily Time Records and/or actual number of workdays rendered.
observations.managementAction: The Management required the PHWs to submit the lacking documentation, and stopped the granting of the fixed rate.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to prepare and properly maintain Subsidiary Ledgers (SL) for those accounts that are composed of several specific accounts/category/accounting responsibility; make sure that beginning balances carried forward in a particular year in the SL should be the same with the ending balances in the previous year; utilize the accounting system to assure correctness, reliability, completeness, and timeliness in recording government financial transactions and to generate financial reports in accordance with the policies and procedures of the NGAS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant have started maintaining SL of the accounts using the ECPAC Accounting System.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the MENRO-Designate to persistently follow-up the NSWMC regarding the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality and demand the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to support the MENRO-Designate in the implementation of the solid waste programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality already have followed-up the NSWMC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the MENRO to: Strictly observe the waste segregation at source and segregated collection by intensive information and education campaign and imposing penalties/fines to violators; fully implement the ordinance on the prohibition on the use of single-use bags and promoting the use of eco bags; assist the barangays in fully establishing and utilizing their MRF by providing necessary support and persistent follow-ups to the DENR for the lifting of the closure order of Cease and Desist Order issued by them to start the operation of the newly constructed MRF and RCA in order to fully maximize its intended environmental purpose.
observations.managementAction: 1. Strict waste segregation is currently implemented; 2. Stores are already advised to lessen or not use plastic bags; 3. Currently coordinating with the barangays.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Accounting Office agreed to: a. Review and counter check the work of HRMO particularly in the review of computation of tax from compensation and; b. Help the HRMO to prepare and submit a report to BIR on the over withheld taxes from compensation and request if a refund can still be made to the affected employees through monthly deduction from the remittable amount of taxes per month.
observations.managementAction: The concerned offices of the Municipality have been coordinating for the proper computation of taxes withheld.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instructs the Municipal Accountant to facilitate the request for write-off by adhering to the specific guidelines and procedures as prescribed by the aforementioned circular to achieve fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Reached out to family members of deceased employees for the requirements to write off, but no request of write-off was received as they encountered difficulties in compiling the relevant required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Sangguniang Bayan through the Vice-Mayor revisits the pertinent provisions of the Articles 107 and 417 of the IRR of RA 7160. The Budget Officer submits the realigned budget to support the disbursements of those four projects and finally, the Budget Officer and the Planning and Development Officer coordinate with the Local Chief Executive to formulate priority development projects.
observations.managementAction: The Budget Officer submitted the realigned budget to support the disbursements of those four projects, and the concerned offices have already been coordinating.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to requires the Municipal Accountant to submit the financial statements and its schedules for the period January 1 to June 30, 2019 and to strictly observe the timely submission thereof, improve recordkeeping and documentation and effectively implement the internal control system. Otherwise, enforce sanctions for non-compliance thereof.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office made some submissions but they were not timely.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Head of the Procuring Entity strictly adheres with the guidelines on the preparation and submission of APP-CSE to the DBM-PS and task the BAC Secretariat to prepare and submit the required APP-CSE for FY 2020 on or before August 31, 2019 in prescribed format to give the DBM-PS an ample time to purchase the needed CSEs and would be prioritized in the procurement of CSEs which is mandated by the Section 53.5 of the IRR of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The Head of the Procuring Entity strictly adhered with the guidelines on the preparation and submission of APP-CSE to the DBM-PS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to adheres strictly the Government Procurement Law and properly accomplish the procurement documents. Entrusts all procurement activities to the Municipal Treasurer or the GSO, as the case may be, and the BAC and not with the requesting office.
observations.managementAction: There were still deficiencies noted on their procurement documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: a. Submit immediately the 13 DVs and its related supporting documents and the deficient necessary supporting documents of the 20 DVs; and b. Requires the Municipal Accountant not to process the DVs with incomplete documentation and to maintain Logbook for all out-going and incoming documents for control purposes to ensure that all documents of the agency will be properly safeguarded against possible loss and unauthorized access.
observations.managementAction: They have started looking for the missing DVs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Planning and Development Office of the LGU strictly considers the direction of the Local Chief Executive in drafting those Annual Investment Programs and in coordination with the Budget Office for the inclusion on its regular and realigned budgets. Further, the Engineering Office shall strictly monitor the implementation of priority developmental projects under the 20% Local Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Office of the LGU strictly considered the direction of the Local Chief Executive in drafting those Annual Investment Programs and in coordination with the Budget Office for the inclusion on its regular and realigned budgets. Further, the Engineering Office strictly monitored the implementation of priority developmental projects under the 20% Local Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous accounting entries in the GF books and establish the Trust Liability-DRRM in the TF books. Further, they should follow strictly the recording and reporting guidelines for LDRRMF as prescribed in COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant adjusted the entries in order to correct the erroneous recording.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to: 1. stops the practice of splitting the procurement of various goods and services; and 2. instructs the General Services Officer or Municipal Treasurer, as the case maybe, and the BAC Chairman to consolidate the procurement of similar and related items into one purchase request and subject the same to competitive bidding pursuant to Rule IV, Section 10 of the revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure competitiveness and obtain the lowest and most advantageous price.
observations.managementAction: The Management have stopped the practice of splitting the procurement of various goods and services.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Budget Office to delegate tasks properly in order to regularly monitor the spending against a specific appropriation in order to avoid incurrence of expenditures in excess or out of the budget.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Office have delegated tasks properly in order to regularly monitor the spending against a specific appropriation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to refrain from entrusting the procurement process to the requesting office for goods not subject to public bidding. All procurement activities should be delegated to the Municipal Treasurer or the GSO, as the case may be, and the BAC.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer, GSO, and BAC Chairman were already informed and made aware of the issue.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accounting Office to observe complete recording of all financial transactions in the books of accounts and immediately make necessary adjustments on the identified erroneous accounting entries.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant adjusted the entries in order to correct the erroneous recording.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. observe proper usage of accounts for every transaction and events; and b. prepare accounting entries to correct the improper journal entries made.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant observed the proper usage of accounts for every transaction and events, and adjusted to correct the accounting entries.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Engineering office to: a. perform detailed pre-engineering activities in conformance with Annex A of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 for all their future infrastructure projects to avoid unreasonable delay in project completion; b. submit immediately to COA the approved suspension orders, requests for time extensions and Statement of Time Elapsed; and c. consider the imposition of liquidated damages to the concerned contractor.
observations.managementAction: The management is still on the process of collecting the imposed liquidated damages. They performed a detailed pre-engineering activities for all their future infrastructure projects to avoid unreasonable delay in project completion but was not able to submit to COA the approved suspension orders, requests for time extensions and Statement of Time Elapsed; and the imposition of liquidated damages to the concerned contractor.

Office and Municipal Treasury Office to perform the required reconciliation at
least quarterly; and the Accounting Office to prepare the Bank Reconciliation
Statements (BRS) for all the funds/bank accounts maintained by the
Municipality with the authorized depository banks every month duly
reconciled with the cashbooks of the Treasury Office and submit the same to
the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

accounts maintained by the Municipality with the authorized depository banks
every month duly reconciled with the cashbooks of the Treasury Office and
submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

1. the Municipal Accountant stops the practice of using expense method in
recording supplies and materials purchases, maintain an inventory account
using perpetual inventory system and prepare supplies ledger card per stock
number;
2. the Municipal Treasurer or the designated Municipal GSO should
maintain supplies and materials stock cards per stock number and prepare
Requisition and Issue Slips and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for recording purposes
to fairly present its inventory accounts in the financial statements; and
3. create a new Inventory Committee and tasked them to conduct the required
semi-annual physical count, reconcile the same with the stock cards and
supplies ledger cards and submit the RPCI to the auditor concerned not
later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second
semesters, respectively.

a. designate a Municipal General Services Officer;
b. instruct the designated Municipal GSO to maintain property cards and
issue acknowledgement receipt for equipment upon issuance of the same in
addition to her tasks expressly recommended in the previous AOM for
Supplies and Materials;
c. instruct the Municipal Accountant to train one of her staff who will be
tasked to maintain the required PPE Ledger Card; and
d. instruct the Inventory Committee to convene and assure its commitment to
religiously exhaust all available resources and conduct the required annual
physical count of plant, property and equipment duly documented through
incorporating in the Report on Physical Count on Property, Plant and
Equipment.

5.11 We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accounting
Office to observe complete recording of all financial transactions in the books
of accounts and immediately make necessary adjustments on the identified
erroneous accounting entries.

2.10	We recommend the Local Chief Executive ensure that the Local Chief Executive ensure the proper granting and utilization of all cash advances as provided in Section 8 of COA Circular No. 97-002. 3.4	We recommend Management to require the Municipal Treasurer to record the financial transactions of the Municipality daily pursuant to Sections 26, 27 and 28 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II. We recommended and Management agreed to strictly adhere with the provisions of COA, DBM, DILG, Governance Commission for GOCCs and DND Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2015-01 dated January 8, 2015.

We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to stop the granting of additional cash advance for Confidential Fund to the Municipal Mayor or Special Disbursing Officer unless the liquidation of previous cash advances have been submitted to the COA-ICFAU for audit.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that all barangay shares from the Real Property and Community Tax Certificate collections are remitted to the LGUs concerned within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 271(d) of RA 7160.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the HRMO to furnish the Audit Team with copies of contract for JOs within five days after its approval in order to facilitate review thereof. Ensure that conditions are clearly stated in the contracts of Service and specify the following: a) expected outputs to facilitate comparison with the accomplishment report; b) specific period within which the work is to be accomplished; and c) source of funds for the payment of salaries.

We recommended that Management require the said personnel to refund the amount paid for his claims for travel expenses. Stop the practice of allowing them to attend trainings and seminars and claim travel expenses. Further, refrain from granting travel expenses to job order personnel that are not urgent and extremely necessary, it does not involve the minimum expenditure and are not beneficial to the agency concerned and/or the country which may result to depletion of LGU resources, increased MOOE and wastage of government funds.

We further recommended that Management stop the practice of granting of overtime payments to job orders which activities  do not warrant the rendition of overtime services. Strictly adhere to Civil Service Commission (CSC) and Department of Budget and Management (DBM) Joint Circular Nos. 1 s. 2015 dated November 25, 2015, No. 2 s. 2004 and No. 2-A s. 2005.

We recommended and the BAC agreed to conduct pre-procurement and pre-bid conferences or as need arises to clarify terms and conditions and specifications stipulated in the bidding documents. The Committee should set or create a shortlist of suppliers and contractors as a guide to canvassers to attend and observe the proceedings of the conference and special meetings to enhance transparency, in compliance with the implementing rules and regulations of RA 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly comply with the posting of information on all infrastructure projects for implementation in a tarpaulin signboard suitably framed for outdoor display at the project location and in accordance to the specification provided for in Item 2.2.3 of COA Circular No. 2013-004 for purposes of promoting transparency and accountability in the use of public funds.

We recommended and Management agreed to adhere strictly to the provisions of Philippine Commission on Women, Department of Interior and Local Government, Department of Budget and Management and National Economic and Development Authority Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. GAD Focal Point System shall be established tasked to promote gender-responsive governance, protect and fulfill women’s human rights, and promote women’s economic development.

We recommended that Management create an Inspection/Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of the PPE owned by the municipality and submit the Report on the Physical Count of the Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Auditor pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1 in order for us to determine the validity and existence of the PPEs owned by the Municipality. Moreover, instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Property Officer to maintain, reconcile and update the records of the PPE.

We recommended that Management implement minimum and standard requirements for segregation and storage of solid waste pending collections and their final disposal to the dumpsite, as follows: a) separate containers/receptacles for all types of wastes shall be maintained from all sources; b) these containers must be properly marked or identified for on-site collections such as   may be determined by the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board; c) maintain receptacles and Materials Recovery Facility for temporary placement of solid wastes; and d) convert the open dump to a controlled dump site.
